# Questions in Livestream 206

Live Stream #206: Getting All Wet

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/PBp54FqPv7E?t=534" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:54</a> | Is it normal for a Protestant to affirm that Peter was the first pope? | He said some Protestants do say that, but the later exclusive Roman sense of "pope" is a historical development. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/PBp54FqPv7E?t=732" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:12</a> | Did Jephthah sacrifice his daughter or dedicate her to religious service? | He argued for an actual sacrifice, noting that the text uses the language of a burnt offering and that Jephthah had legal alternatives he did not use. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/PBp54FqPv7E?t=995" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:35</a> | Is there any literal chronology to Judges? | He said no clean literal chronology can be extracted because Judges is arranged in overlapping, didactic fashion rather than simple sequence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/PBp54FqPv7E?t=2731" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:31</a> | What book would you recommend instead of G. K. Beale on the temple? | He recommended J. Daniel Hays's *The Temple and the Tabernacle*. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/PBp54FqPv7E?t=2820" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:00</a> | Do Matthew 1:23 and Matthew 28:20 connect? | Yes. He said Matthew begins with Emmanuel, "God with us," and ends with Jesus promising to be with his people always. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/PBp54FqPv7E?t=2924" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">48:44</a> | If Jesus is our Passover lamb, how is he our Yom Kippur bull or goat? | He said the Day of Atonement animals covered sins typologically, but Christ fulfills the sacrificial system at a deeper level than those offerings could. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/PBp54FqPv7E?t=3083" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:23</a> | Why doesn't Christianity celebrate Yom Kippur? | He said Yom Kippur covered national rebellion, while Christians believe Christ's blood fully forgives and pays the debt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/PBp54FqPv7E?t=3240" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:00</a> | Was Abraham a polytheist? | He said it is hard to prove; at minimum Abraham owed allegiance to one God, and before his call he may have been polytheistic or henotheistic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/PBp54FqPv7E?t=3363" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:03</a> | Would Abraham be considered Christian? | Strictly, not during his lifetime before the incarnation, but he trusted the promise of Christ and the afterlife question depends on relation to Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/PBp54FqPv7E?t=3463" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:43</a> | How do we "bless God" in Deuteronomy 8:10? | He treated it as praise and thanksgiving offered back to God after receiving his gifts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/PBp54FqPv7E?t=4978" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:58</a> | In Leviticus 27:28-29, what is a man set apart for destruction? | He identified this as herem, a devoted-to-destruction category tied to interclan vendetta where persons or goods could not be redeemed. |
